subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Hertzsprung-Russell Diagram
description: A 60-second lesson on The Hertzsprung-Russell Diagram.

hook.text: One chart maps the life story of every star. The H-R diagram plots brightness against temperature.

buildup.text: Most stars sit on a diagonal band called the main sequence—hot blue stars at top left, cool red ones at bottom right.

discovery.text: Ejnar Hertzsprung and Henry Russell created it independently around 1910. It became astronomy's most powerful tool.

twist.text: As stars age, they move off the main sequence. Red giants drift upper-right; white dwarfs fall to the lower-left.

climax.text: By plotting a cluster on the diagram, astronomers can determine its age with remarkable precision.

punchline.text: One chart, every star's biography. Temperature and brightness tell all.

quiz.question: What does the Hertzsprung-Russell diagram plot?
